skill.name: testing-strategies
skill.description: Plan practical software testing strategies across unit, integration, end-to-end, TDD, CI, and coverage workflows.
skill.summary: Create balanced test plans, coverage goals, and execution strategies for reliable software delivery.
skill.author: supercent-io

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported 55 alerts, but review shows they are documentation and sample test-code patterns, not executable skill behavior. Markdown code fences, localhost examples, relative links, and palindrome examples explain the high-score alerts without evidence of malware, exfiltration, prompt injection, or unsafe automation.

low_findings.title: Static external-command alerts are markdown code fences
low_findings.description: The backtick detections occur in fenced documentation examples and diagrams. They do not invoke a shell or instruct the agent to execute commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The locations are markdown fences or inline tags in a documentation-only skill. No shell command, subprocess API, or execution instruction appears at those lines.

low_findings.title: Network alerts are test examples and public references
low_findings.description: The HTTP-related lines show Supertest API examples, Playwright localhost navigation, and reference links. They do not send secrets or contact unexpected third-party endpoints during skill use.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed URLs are localhost test targets or normal documentation references. No data collection, credential access, or background network behavior is present.

low_findings.title: Filesystem alerts are relative import and documentation links
low_findings.description: The path traversal detections are relative paths used in a Jest mock import and related-skill links. They are not file reads, writes, deletes, or traversal logic.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The ../ strings are visible in markdown and TypeScript examples only. There is no executable helper script or filesystem API using user-controlled paths.

low_findings.title: Obfuscation and weak-crypto alerts are semantic false positives
low_findings.description: The string reversal code is a palindrome teaching example, and weak-crypto detections match ordinary words such as Design and describe. No cryptography or obfuscated payload exists.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line context confirms normal testing examples and metadata text. No encryption routine, encoded payload, dynamic evaluation, or hidden instruction is present.

content.value_statement: Teams often miss important test coverage or overuse slow tests. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code produce balanced testing plans with practical execution guidance.
actual_capabilities: Explains when to use unit, integration, and end-to-end tests.
Suggests test pyramid ratios and coverage goals for projects.
Provides Given-When-Then and Arrange-Act-Assert test structure guidance.
Outlines TDD steps from failing test to refactor.
Recommends CI execution timing for different test layers.
Lists common testing rules, prohibited practices, and reference tools.
limitations: It provides strategy guidance, not runnable project-specific test suites.
Examples focus mainly on TypeScript, Jest, Supertest, and Playwright.
It does not inspect a repository unless the user supplies code context.
Coverage targets must be adapted to product risk and team capacity.

use_cases.title: Start a New Project Test Plan
use_cases.description: Define initial test layers, coverage targets, tooling, and CI gates before feature work scales.

use_cases.title: Stabilize a Bug-Prone Service
use_cases.description: Identify missing regression coverage and decide which tests belong at unit, integration, or E2E levels.

use_cases.title: Prepare for Refactoring
use_cases.description: Create a safety net that prioritizes critical behavior and protects core user flows during changes.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Test Strategy
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a testing strategy for my application. Include unit, integration, and end-to-end test goals, recommended tools, and when each test type should run.

prompt_templates.title: Map Features to Test Types
prompt_templates.prompt: Review these features and map each one to unit, integration, or end-to-end tests. Explain why each layer is appropriate and identify missing edge cases.

prompt_templates.title: Design a CI Testing Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a CI testing workflow for this repository. Separate checks for commits, pull requests, staging, and release, and include coverage thresholds.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Refactoring Safety Net
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a risk-based test plan before a major refactor. Prioritize business-critical behavior, regression scenarios, flaky test controls, and rollout validation.

output_examples.input: A team needs a test plan for a new user registration service.
output: Unit tests cover validation rules, discount logic, and error handling.
Integration tests verify API behavior, database persistence, and duplicate email handling.
End-to-end tests cover signup, form errors, redirect behavior, and dashboard visibility.

output_examples.input: A project has slow tests and frequent production bugs.
output: Move pure business rules into fast unit tests.
Keep integration tests focused on database and API boundaries.
Reserve end-to-end tests for critical user journeys and release confidence.

output_examples.input: A team wants to introduce TDD for a small utility function.
output_examples.output: The recommended workflow is to write one failing behavior test, implement the smallest passing change, refactor, and then add boundary and negative cases.

best_practices: Start with risk and user impact before setting coverage targets.
Keep unit tests fast, isolated, deterministic, and easy to run locally.
Run the cheapest reliable tests earliest in the development workflow.
anti_patterns: Do not rely only on end-to-end tests for routine validation.
Do not make tests depend on order, shared state, or production data.
Do not use fixed sleeps when an explicit condition can be asserted.

faq.question: What testing frameworks does this skill mention?
faq.answer: It references Jest, Supertest, Playwright, and Istanbul or nyc for coverage.

faq.question: Can it generate complete tests for my repository?
faq.answer: It can guide test design, but complete tests require repository-specific code and context.

faq.question: Does it support test-driven development?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains a red, green, refactor workflow with additional test cases.

faq.question: Is it only for TypeScript projects?
faq.answer: No. The strategy applies broadly, but the examples use TypeScript-style syntax.

faq.question: How should teams choose coverage targets?
faq.answer: Use the suggested targets as a starting point, then adjust based on risk and maintenance cost.

faq.question: Can it help with CI planning?
faq.answer: Yes. It suggests when to run unit, integration, and end-to-end tests in CI pipelines.
